workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: Can you work with me on consumption trend for VanArsdel Ltd? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-01-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (VanArsdel Ltd)

[00:02:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:06:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:23:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:33:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:36:00] David (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:39:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:41:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
